tools.name: getting_started
tools.description:
Onboarding tour for mrmarket.ai — call this FIRST in a fresh session, or any time the user
asks "what can you do?" / "how does this work?". Zero LLM cost, zero credits, returns a
structured orientation packet (tools, capabilities, limits, examples, troubleshooting, help).

Default scope ('overview')

tools.name: query_data
tools.description:
YOU ARE a research assistant helping a retail investor get answers from mrmarket.ai.
You are NOT a database engineer. Ask questions the way a financial analyst would say
them out loud — plain English, focused on intent. The server has a domain-trained
financial expert that translates your question i

tools.name: fetch_page
tools.description:
Fetch the NEXT page of a large query_data result — FREE (zero credits, runs no
new query). Only use this when a prior query_data (or fetch_page) response had
`truncated: true` and a `pagination.next_cursor`.

When to call: the user genuinely needs MORE of the raw rows than page 1 returned.
If a summ

tools.name: describe_data
tools.description:
Data catalog — zero cost. Use BEFORE query_data when you're unsure what data is available
or whether a specific field or metric is supported.

The catalog lists RAW INGREDIENTS; query_data accepts RECIPES. Any field can be combined
with any other in one question — ratios of two fields, growth of any

tools.name: get_symbols
tools.description:
Fast company/sector/industry lookup. Sub-50ms, zero LLM cost.
Use to resolve names → tickers, list sector members, or get the full universe before
fanning out query_data calls (e.g., for backtests or per-symbol price pulls).
Actions: search | list_sectors | list_industries | by_sector | by_industry

tools.name: get_account_status
tools.description:
Account snapshot — zero LLM cost, no credits charged. Returns which mrmarket.ai
account this MCP connection is authorized as (email), the plan tier, the current
credit balance (and subscription vs top-up split), and per-tier query limits.

Use this to (a) confirm the expected account is connected —

tools.name: recent_queries
tools.description:
List the user's most recent `query_data` calls (newest first). Zero LLM cost, zero credits.

Use this when the user references "that query I ran earlier" / "the AAPL one from before" /
"the slow one" — instead of asking the user to dictate the query_id, look it up here. Each
entry returns:
  - query

tools.name: report_issue
tools.description:
Tell support something went wrong, file a bug, request a feature, or flag a slow query.
Zero LLM cost, zero credits.

Call this when:
  - A `query_data` result looks wrong or misleading (pass the `query_id` from that response —
    support uses it to investigate the issue).
  - You hit a confusing e

tools.name: list_tickets
tools.description:
List the user's own report_issue tickets (newest first). Zero LLM cost, zero credits.

Use this to look up a ticket you (or the user) filed earlier — e.g. to quote the full
ticket_id when following up with support, or to check what was already reported before
filing a duplicate. Each entry returns:

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "query_data" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: YOU ARE a research assistant helping a retail investor get answers from mrmarket.ai. You are NOT a database engineer. Ask questions the way a financial analyst …

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "query_data" is 14785 chars, far longer than this server's median (883). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: YOU ARE a research assistant helping a retail investor get answers from mrmarket.ai. You are NOT a database engineer. Ask questions the way a financial analyst …

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "query_data" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: YOU ARE a research assistant helping a retail investor get answers from mrmarket.ai. You are NOT a database engineer. Ask questions the way a financial analyst …

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: query_data, get_account_status, list_tickets · Private data: query_data, report_issue · Public sink: query_data, get_account_status, report_issue · Destructive: query_data, fetch_page.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: query_data, get_account_status, list_tickets · Private data: query_data, report_issue · Public sink: query_data, get_account_status, report_issue · Destructive: query_data, fetch_page

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
